dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for chest pain at 1234 Cherry Lane in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Steve, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I had chest pain on my left side, a 9 on a 10 scale, sharp and without any provocation.
When did the chest pain start? Have you had it before?
Can you tell me your name, what today is, and where you are right now, please?
Are you alert, verbal, painful, or unresponsive right now, sir?
My name is Marcus. I'm in Richmond. And it's 2020.
What caused you to call 911 today, sir?
I had a sharp chest pain and was hearing voices telling me to hurt myself.
You're alert and oriented to time, place, and person. That's good to hear.
Airway is patent. He's speaking clearly and calmly.
His breathing sounds normal. Can I listen to your breath sounds now, sir?
Respirations are 16, regular with breath sounds clear and equal on both sides.
I'll check your pulse and skin condition now. One moment please.
Blood pressure is 106/70.
Heart rate is 119, elevated but regular.
Oxygen saturation is 99%, which is normal.
Breathing is 16 per minute, with no signs of labored effort.
Marcus, do you have any medications you take daily? Can you tell me what they are?
I take Haldol, Cogentin, and Lisinopril for bipolar, schizoaffective, and high blood pressure.
Do you have any known allergies to medications or food?
No, no known allergies to medications or food.
When did the hallucinations about seeing ghosts and hearing voices start?
It started about three days ago - I see ghosts and hear voices telling me to harm myself.
What activity, food, or substance did you consume in the last 24 hours, sir?
I smoked crack about an hour ago when the chest pain started. That's when the hallucinations got worse.
Any recent injuries or falls, sir? We see the police are speaking with you now.
No, I don't have any recent injuries or falls. I was just out with police for a behavior issue.
Have you had a fever or chills in the last 24 hours?
No, I feel normal aside from chest pain and the hallucinations.
Have you had any stomach pain or discomfort recently?
No, I haven't had any stomach discomfort or digestive issues.
Have you had any cough or chest discomfort with breathing today?
No, no cough or breathing discomfort.
Shortness of breath or difficulty catching your breath?
No, my breathing is fine at the moment.

dialogue.micro_intent: assess for anxiety or panic attack
dialogue.utterance: Have you had a history of anxiety or chest pain from other causes like panic attacks before?

dialogue.micro_intent: assess for anxiety or panic attack
dialogue.utterance: No, I haven't - the chest pain is new.

dialogue.micro_intent: assess for recent exposure to COVID-19
dialogue.utterance: Close contact with anyone recently who might have had the virus or been infected?

dialogue.micro_intent: assess for recent exposure to COVID-19
dialogue.utterance: No, I haven't been near anyone suspected or confirmed for the virus.
Has the chest pain gotten worse, stayed the same, or improved since it started?
It just started and has stayed at a sharp 9 - no change in the pain level so far.
Does anything make the chest pain better or worse, like position, breathing, or movement?
No, it comes and goes randomly and is sharp on the left.
Skin is warm and dry, no abnormal coloration or paleness observed.
Pupils are equal and reactive at 4mm, and face is symmetrical.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Any drainage or pain from your nose or ears?

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Patient
dialogue.utterance: No, I don't have issues with my nose or ears.
Chest wall is stable with equal rise on both sides.
Your abdomen is soft and non-tender with no distention.
Everything in my stomach is fine - it's soft and normal.
No deformities, swelling, or weakness in the arms or legs.
Blood glucose is normal - you're safe for transport at this point.
EKG shows sinus tachycardia with signs of early repolarization and no other ectopy seen.
Let me start with 4-lead monitoring as a precaution.
We'll just go with the 4-lead for now. The 12-lead EKG couldn't be performed.
We'll administer 324 milligrams of Aspirin now as part of our chest pain protocol.
Okay, go ahead. I'm fine with that.
Based on the protocol 2-1, we're managing for possible cardiac issues with your history and symptoms.
Your pulse is now 103, and blood pressure is up to 125/74 - showing some improvement.
Oxygen saturation remains stable at 97% - we are clear for transport now.
Glasgow Coma Scale remains at 15 - you're awake, alert, and answering well.
Would you prefer to be taken to Chippenham Hospital for evaluation and care, sir?
Yes, I want to go to Chippenham for treatment and be seen as soon as possible.
We'll position you semi-Fowler on the stretcher with your shoulders and lap stable for transport.
Got you all set and stable, Marcus. You're safe for transport now.
ED has been alerted. You will be taken to room 12 upon arrival.
Noting sharp-onset chest pain as a 9/10, with history of psychiatric disorders and crack use confirmed.
ASA administered and 4-lead monitoring in use. No signs of MI on ECG.
Scene is clear. Let's get stable and on the way. Arrival at hospital expected shortly.
